title: Kortex status cache
description: Snapshot of repo state consumed by bridge-in and health to avoid re-walking the filesystem on every invocation. Written by scripts/commands/regen-status.mjs (invoked from .husky/post-commit and as a manual `pnpm kortex regen-status`). Lives at .cache/status.json. Schema is intentionally minimal — only fields with active consumers are included; new fields are added when a consumer needs them, not speculatively.

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this snapshot was written. Consumers may warn if older than ~24h, but the cache is not invalidated on age — staleness is a write-side concern (post-commit regenerates).

priorities.description: Active priorities parsed from AGENTS.md `### Active Priorities` numbered list. Surfaced in bridge-in state block so the model sees current focus without re-reading AGENTS.md sections.

stale_count.description: Count of wiki/ pages whose `updated:` is older than 60 days AND whose `distillation_level:` is < 2. Same definition as health.checkStaleWiki — keep both in sync if the threshold changes.

open_handoffs.description: Count of session files in output/sessions/ that contain a `## Handoff HH:MM` section without a later `## Bridge-out HH:MM`. Indicates sessions that paused mid-flight and were never closed.
